ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: Skill certification scheme aiming to enable Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training.
benefits: Free skill training courses aligned with NSQF
Assessment and certification by recognized bodies
Placement assistance and soft skills mentoring
Monetary rewards for certified candidates

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: Scheme to promote apprenticeship training and increase the engagement of apprentices in industries by sharing stipends.
benefits: On-the-job training in recognized industries
Sharing of 25% of prescribed stipend up to Rs 1,500 per month
Apprenticeship training certificate upon completion

description: Provides 12-month internship opportunities to youth in India's top 500 companies to gain corporate exposure.
education: Higher Secondary / ITI / Diploma / Undergraduates (BA, BSc, BCom, BBA, BPharmacy)
income_limit: Family income must not exceed Rs 8,00,000 per annum
benefits: Monthly stipend of Rs 5,000 for 12 months
One-time financial assistance of Rs 6,000 for local requirements
Insurance coverage under PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana and PM Suraksha Bima Yojana

scheme_name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
description: Placement-linked skill training program targeted exclusively at rural poor youth.
income_limit: Below Poverty Line (BPL) or low-income household
benefits: Completely free skill training programs
Free fooding, lodging, and uniform for residential courses
Assured placement in salaried jobs (minimum 70% placement rate)
Post-placement support for one year

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: Creates an enabling ecosystem for entrepreneurship development through education, training, and mentorship.
benefits: Structured entrepreneurship education courses
Access to startup mentorship and industry networks
Incubation support and links to credit agencies

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship Scheme for SC Students
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: Financial assistance to SC students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary courses to enable them to complete their education.
education: Post-matric (Class 11, 12, Graduate, Postgraduate, or Professional degrees)
benefits: Full tuition fee reimbursement
Monthly maintenance allowance up to Rs 1,200 depending on course category
Additional allowances for disabled students
required_documents: Income Certificate issued by competent authority

description: Awarded to meritorious students of economically weaker sections to prevent them from dropping out at class VIII and encourage secondary education.
education: Class VIII pass with minimum 55% marks, currently studying in Class IX
benefits: Scholarship of Rs 12,000 per annum (Rs 1,000 per month) from Class IX to Class XII

description: Scheme to assist and support girl students in technical education by providing financial aid.
education: Admitted to 1st year Degree/Diploma program in AICTE approved institutions
benefits: Rs 50,000 per annum for tuition fees, computer purchase, books, and stationeries
No separate maintenance allowance is paid
required_documents: Declaration by parents that family income is within limits

scheme_name: National Scheme for Incentive to Girls for Secondary Education (NSIGSE)
description: Incentive scheme to establish an enabling environment for secondary enrollment and retention of girl child.
education: Passed Class VIII and enrolled in Class IX of Government/Government-aided schools
benefits: A sum of Rs 3,000 is deposited as a fixed deposit in the girl's bank account
Accumulated interest and principal amount can be withdrawn on completing 18 years of age and passing class X

description: Scheme to incentivize employers for generating new employment by contributing full employer's EPF/EPS share.
income_limit: Monthly wages must be less than Rs 15,000
benefits: Government pays the full 12% employer contribution towards EPF and EPS for 3 years
Enhances workers' social security benefits and net take-home salary
required_documents: Universal Account Number (UAN) linked with Aadhaar

description: Incentivizes employers to recruit new employees during the post-pandemic recovery phase by providing social security subsidies.
income_limit: Monthly salary must be less than Rs 15,000
benefits: Subsidy of both employee's (12% of wages) and employer's (12% of wages) EPF contributions for establishments employing up to 1000 employees
Subsidy of only employee's EPF contribution for establishments employing more than 1000 employees

description: A digital portal offering job matching, career guidance, skills evaluation, and info on job fairs.
benefits: Free job placement and matching services
Access to thousands of job postings across public and private sectors
Free career counselling, aptitude testing, and training linkages

scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
description: Livelihood security program providing at least 100 days of guaranteed wage employment to rural households.
benefits: 100 days of guaranteed unskilled manual labour employment per household per year
Statutory minimum wage payouts direct to bank account
Unemployment allowance if work is not allotted within 15 days of demand

scheme_name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
description: Provides regular financial assistance to meritorious but economically poor students for post-school expenses.
education: Passed Class 12 and in the top 20th percentile, currently pursuing regular higher studies
benefits: Scholarship of Rs 12,000 per annum for the first three years of graduation
Scholarship of Rs 20,000 per annum during postgraduate studies

scheme_name: SANKALP (Skills Acquisition and Knowledge Awareness for Livelihood Promotion)
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: Skill strengthening program targeting quality assurance, trainers development, and gender-inclusive placements.
benefits: Access to industry-aligned certified vocational training
Improved institutional support and localized skill opportunities
Special incentives for marginalized and female trainees

scheme_name: STRIVE (Skills Strengthening for Industrial Value Enhancement)
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: World Bank-funded program to improve performance of ITIs and expand apprenticeship training within local industry clusters.
education: ITI Marksheet or 8th/10th class pass (enrolled in ITIs)
benefits: Access to modern workshops, updated curriculums, and industry trainers
Direct industry linkages for industrial apprenticeship placement

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: Skill development program for Scheduled Castes, OBCs, EBCs, and sanitation workers to improve earning capacity.
income_limit: OBC families: less than Rs 3,00,000 p.a. | EBC families: less than Rs 1,00,000 p.a. | SC/Sanitation Workers: No limit
benefits: Free skill training (Short-term, Long-term, or Entrepreneurship)
Stipend of Rs 1,000 to Rs 1,500 per month depending on attendance
Placement linkages and certification

description: Skill development scheme for youth belonging to notified minority communities to ensure employability.
benefits: Fully funded residential and non-residential skill development courses
Monthly stipend for training period
Assured job placements for at least 75% of certified candidates

description: Leadership and digital literacy training program for women belonging to minority communities.
benefits: Free training on leadership, financial literacy, life skills, and civic rights
Access to community networks and government-support linkages

description: A single-window platform to search and apply for verified internship placements across sectors.
education: Enrolled in Diploma, Graduation, or Postgraduate programs
benefits: Access to verified internships in Corporates, MSMEs, and Government agencies
Internship certificate issued by AICTE and hiring organization
Opportunities for performance-linked stipends
